question.text: A company with paid-up capital of Rs. 8 crore and market capitalization of Rs. 30 crore applies for listing on NSE. The company has been profitable for the last 4 years and the issue size is Rs. 12 crore. Which eligibility criterion is NOT met?

answer.text: Minimum paid-up capital requirement of Rs. 10 crore

explanation.text: **The Logic:** NSE requires: paid-up capital of at least Rs. 10 crore, track record of profitability of at least 3 years, and market cap of at least Rs. 25 crore. This company meets profitability (4 years > 3 years) and market cap (Rs. 30 crore > Rs. 25 crore) but fails the paid-up capital test (Rs. 8 crore < Rs. 10 crore). **The Trap:** The company's market cap of Rs. 30 crore exceeds the Rs. 25 crore threshold, so candidates might focus on that and overlook the paid-up capital shortfall. Always check ALL eligibility criteria systematically. **Cross-Exam Context:** Listing eligibility norms are tested in NISM Series IX and understanding capital structure is covered in Series XII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When assessing IPO readiness, create a checklist of ALL exchange-specific listing criteria. Companies sometimes plan their capital restructuring specifically to meet the Rs. 10 crore paid-up capital threshold before filing.
